interpretation: The inclusion unit is a complete clean 5-minute market; model-ready coverage now has 849 such markets. Full-day continuity is preferred context, not a hard gate: 0 full 288-window days and 3 days with at least 50% of expected windows; the best day has 259/288 windows. 668 feature rows used source-tagged backup BTC prices. The maker 1c proxy has 30 positive coarse rules, but none pass the day-stability gate, so they remain fragile fill-proxy diagnostics. Queue-aware maker 1c walk-forward is stricter: 0/960 rules pass (0/8640 order/horizon rows; best pooled planned-cost ROI is -6.4%). Keep maker 1c disabled until this gate turns positive. The coarse taker-rule search has 38 pooled holdout-positive rules and 7 day-stable rules. Treat them as candidates for deeper latency-cost modeling, not live edges. With at most one taker entry per market, 73 rule variants are holdout-positive and 36 pass the day-stability gate; the strongest variants still only span 3 test days, so they need more clean-market coverage before sizing. Latency stress keeps 7/7 day-stable taker rules alive at 2s latency with a 2c slippage cap, but 0/7 survive at 10s with the same cap. First-entry execution sizing stress shows 35/36 rules still passing for $5 orders at 2s latency and a 2c cap (average fill rate 69.6%); $25 orders have 21/36 passing (average fill rate 60.8%). This suggests top-of-book size is not the main blocker at these small sizes in the current clean sample. Dense-day walk-forward validation is stricter: 1/36 day-stable first-entry variants pass with up to 2 eligible forward folds. This makes the current edge evidence fragile until more clean dense days are collected. Combined execution walk-forward is the strictest current gate: 16/36 rules pass at least one order/latency/slippage setting (352 parameter rows). At 2s latency and a 2c cap, $5 has 8/8 eligible rules passing and $25 has 8/8. Prior-selected execution policy validation is the closest current bot simulation: 11/11 policies pass across 2 selected forward folds. The best passing policy is order_25_2s_2c_risk_adjusted with pooled planned-cost ROI 11.7%, 74.1% fill rate, and worst-fold ROI 5.1%. This still needs more forward folds. Market-level replay of that selected-policy family has 158 signals, 117 simulated full top-of-book fills, 11.7% planned-cost ROI, and 6.5% max drawdown on planned cost. The day-bootstrap 5th percentile is 5.1%; the research-only per-signal cap is 0.25% while the selected forward-day count stays small.
